rationale: Y is the better overall abstract because it is cleaner, follows Europace-style heading conventions more naturally, and presents the study flow with less compression. X has a slightly more specific gap because it explicitly names AFL under-assessment and unsupervised ambulatory performance, but both abstracts give strong diagnostic metrics with comparator and p value where relevant. Both lose certainty-calibration points because a single prospective validation after cardioversion does not fully justify the broad claim that smartphone PPG can be used as a standalone diagnostic device without confirmatory ECG.
loser_weakness: X most lacked calibrated conclusion wording and polished journal-style presentation; its final sentence overextends beyond the validation design.
